what_it_does: Design document proposing a concurrent-cognition layer over the resident Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ vLLM endpoint at :8000: it budgets ~32 concurrent generation slots between a sequential-part main reply and a swarm of structured-output roles, and injects swarm outputs into the next part via the existing address-resolution context path.
observations: Maps the current synchronous model call path: urllib-based transports in fabric/transport.py, synchronous client wrappers in fabric/client.py, and a single-threaded scheduler/runtime.
Notes the bridge already uses ThreadingHTTPServer for concurrent HTTP requests, but no model calls are issued concurrently.
Recommends ThreadPoolExecutor for the swarm because the existing transport is a stateless blocking callable and Python releases the GIL during socket I/O.
Rejects an asyncio/httpx rewrite to avoid churn across existing string-callers of the transport.
Proposes a shared threading.Semaphore(32) as a global ceiling plus a swarm pool capped at 32-R to reserve slots for the main reply and judge.
Moves the main conversational reply onto the registered resident 4B at :8000 so slot budgeting and prefix-caching evidence apply coherently.
Warns against silent fallback to the cloud brain when the 4B is not resident.
Reuses resolve_role and ROLE_REGISTRY to bind swarm roles, with structured JSON output enforced by schema.
Reuses _resolve_context_at and the _chat_context append seam at suite.py:1461 as the injection mechanism for swarm outputs.
Defines a swarm://<turn>/<role> address namespace where roles write JSON and later parts resolve it.
Cites benchmark-sheet evidence for the c=32 sweet spot, prefix caching, and reliable json_schema output.
Lists open design questions for Tim: reserved-slot count, part-boundary trigger, initial role cast, residency policy, namespace GC, cloud escape hatch, and tools array across parts.
standouts: The two-layer slot budget: a global Semaphore(32) ceiling plus a swarm pool self-capped at 32-R to reserve foreground slots.
Moving the main chat reply onto the resident 4B so the whole concurrency model is internally consistent.
Reusing the existing address-resolution context path instead of inventing a new injection mechanism.
conventions: Evidence-based design with file:line citations and benchmark-sheet references.
Registry-is-truth, fail-loud, additive change, and off-the-agent-face principles.
Reuse of existing role-registry and address-resolution patterns.
Structured JSON schema outputs for inter-role contracts.
concerns: The design concentrates the main reply, judge, and swarm on a single resident 4B endpoint; if that endpoint is not loaded, the mode has no graceful fallback unless it fails loud.
Turning chat() into a sequential part-loop adds latency and complexity whose tuning is left open.
The cloud-brain fallback question is flagged but not resolved.
Concrete implementation details for the swarm namespace gather branch and garbage collection are not specified.
notes: A design brief that turns the resident 4B into a time-sliced stage: foreground sequential reply parts and background concurrent role waves, bridged by address-resolved context.
questions: What exact value of R (reserved slots) will be chosen and how will it be measured?
How are part boundaries decided: fixed count, token budget, or a judge-like boundary detector?
Which role types populate the initial swarm?
What is the residency policy when the 4B is not loaded: fail loud or request a load?
How is the swarm:// namespace garbage-collected or persisted across turns?
Are tool arrays offered on every part or only the final part?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is to design concurrent execution and injection, and the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Concurrency as caller-side composition rather than transport rewrite.
Residency-first fast path with guarded cloud fallback.
Reuse of existing injection and address-resolution mechanisms.
Bounded resource budgeting to prevent foreground starvation.
intention_signals: It is trying to add a parallel 'thinking swarm' alongside the main chat reply without rewriting the synchronous model stack, by slicing the reply into parts and resolving role outputs as context between slices.
novelty: The combination of a process-wide semaphore slot budget, a self-capped swarm pool, and reusing address-resolved context injection to stitch sequential reply parts together.
contribution: A concrete, evidence-backed concurrency-and-injection design that keeps the existing transport, client, role-registry, and address-resolution machinery intact while adding a slot-budgeted swarm and a sequential-part reply loop.
summary_for_embedding: Design document for a concurrent-cognition layer that runs up to 32 structured-output swarm roles on the resident Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ vLLM endpoint between sequential parts of a chat reply, injecting role outputs via the existing address-resolution context path and reserving slots to prevent turn starvation.
intention_for_embedding: To specify how to add parallel resident-model cognition to the chat path without rewriting the synchronous model stack.
